title: FeedbackSchema
description: User-filed feedback on mixed-up-files surfaces. Captured by right-click → Feedback… on any UUID-bearing entity (grid rows, filter chips, column headers, chat preview pane, page header). Persisted into Dolt — never SQLite — so each row gets its own `dolt log` entry via `CALL DOLT_COMMIT('-Am', 'feedback: <uuid>')`. Read-back is out of scope; the user queries Dolt directly. Single source of truth for the row shape (Rust struct + DDL, Python dataclass + DDL, TypeScript types) and for the discriminated-union shape of the per-surface payload that lives inside `context_json`.

FeedbackRow.description: One row in the `feedback` table. Strongly-typed top-level columns hold the things we want indexable (sentiment, when, version/git, the surface discriminator), and `context_json` carries the variable per-surface payload as JSON.

feedback_uuid.description: Client-generated UUIDv4. Used both as the primary key and as the human-readable handle in `dolt log` messages.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp with explicit local offset (per AGENTS.md timestamp convention), stamped server-side at insert time.

sentiment.description: Optional thumb up / down. NULL when the user submitted only a comment without choosing a direction.

comment.description: Free-form user comment. Required and non-empty — Submit is disabled in the UI until this has content.

app_version.description: Backend `CARGO_PKG_VERSION` at the moment the feedback was recorded.

git_hash.description: Git commit SHA the binary was built from, sourced from the Bazel `--workspace_status_command` stamp. Falls back to the literal string "unknown" for non-Bazel builds (`cargo run`).

context_json.description: JSON payload matching `FeedbackContext` (this same file). Serialized at the HTTP boundary; readers parse on demand. Stored as MySQL/Dolt `JSON` so `JSON_EXTRACT` filters are cheap; SQLite's reference impl stores the same JSON as plain text.

fixed_in_git_hash.description: Filled in retrospectively (by hand) when the issue this feedback raised has been addressed: the git SHA of the commit that fixed it. NULL means unresolved. Not server-stamped — the writer at submit time leaves it NULL and a human updates it via a manual UPDATE once the fix lands.

notes.description: Free-form maintainer notes about the resolution: how the fix works, which test exercises this case, follow-ups, etc. Companion to `fixed_in_git_hash`; both are populated by hand after the fact.

FeedbackContext.description: Decoded shape of `FeedbackRow.context_json`. Top-level fields are always present; the typed per-surface payload lives in `payload` and is discriminated by `surface`. Producers fill this in via `frankweiler/ui/src/feedback/context.ts`; consumers should switch on `surface` and treat `payload` as the matching variant.

url.description: `window.location.href` at the moment Submit was clicked.

surface.description: Discriminator for `payload`. Each value selects one of the FeedbackSurface* variants below.

dom_path_breadcrumb.description: Path from the right-clicked element up to the nearest `[data-feedback-root]` (or `<body>`), one step per ancestor. Recorded so the maintainer can rebuild what the user was pointing at even after the DOM structure shifts.

dom_path_selector.description: Flat CSS-selector form of `dom_path_breadcrumb`. Lossy but quick to eyeball.

target_uuids.description: Every UUID the feedback is `about`. Includes any row UUIDs from Lightroom multi-select expansion; for preview surfaces it includes the conversation UUID plus the message UUID(s).

payload.description: Per-surface typed payload. The variant is selected by `surface`. v0 keeps the runtime shape an open object — the cross-language codegen for the tagged union is wired in T10.

DomPathStep.description: One ancestor in `dom_path_breadcrumb`. We record only what's useful for reconstruction — no inline styles, no random attributes.

id.description: Element id when set. Used in the flat selector as `#id`.

classes.description: Class list as seen in the DOM. Order is preserved.

data.description: `data-*` attributes on the element. Used to recover the UUID context (e.g. `data-msg-index`, `data-uuid`).

FeedbackSurfaceGridCell.description: Right-click on a grid cell (a filterable column on a row). Lightroom semantics expand `row_uuids` to the full selection when the right-clicked row is in the active selection; otherwise it contains the single right-clicked row.

column.description: Column field key the user right-clicked (e.g. `source`, `author`, `conversation_name`).

cell_value.description: Displayed text content of the right-clicked cell. Useful when the column has rendered formatting that differs from the underlying value.

FeedbackSurfaceGridRow.description: Right-click on a grid row outside any filterable cell (e.g. the row gutter). Same Lightroom expansion as `grid_cell` but with no column attached.

FeedbackSurfacePreviewMessage.description: Right-click on a single message in the chat preview pane. Used when there is no active text selection.

message_uuid.description: UUID of the message under the cursor — read from the closest `[id^="m-"][data-msg-index]` ancestor.

message_index.description: Zero-based index of the message within its conversation; matches the `data-msg-index` attribute.

FeedbackSurfacePreviewSelection.description: Right-click while a non-empty text selection sits inside the chat preview pane. Captures the selected text plus its anchor / focus message UUIDs.

start_message_uuid.description: Message UUID containing the selection anchor (where the drag began).

end_message_uuid.description: Message UUID containing the selection focus (where the drag ended). Equal to `start_message_uuid` for selections inside one message.

selected_text.description: Result of `window.getSelection().toString()` at submit time. Trimmed lightly — leading/trailing whitespace only — never reflowed.

FeedbackSurfacePageHeader.description: Feedback button on the page-level header (e.g. the `Feedback` affordance next to `CopyUuidButton` in `ChatPreviewPane.vue` / `ChatView.vue`). Used when the user wants to flag something about the entire entity rather than a specific row or message.

entity_kind.description: What kind of entity owns the page. Today only `conversation`; widens as new page types acquire the affordance.

entity_uuid.description: UUID of the page-level entity (e.g. the conversation UUID).

FeedbackSurfaceFilterChip.description: Right-click on one of the filter-bar chips (e.g. `source:Claude`).

value.description: Chip value the user is filtering on.

FeedbackSurfaceColumnHeader.description: Right-click on a column header (e.g. `Author`). Useful when the column's transformation or rendering looks wrong independent of any specific row.
